name: Alchemy
note: Alchemist Vizyl is the alchemy trainer, located in the Blackfeather Bazaar in Night Harbor. Next to him is an alchemist who sells the required flasks and vials, and will buy back excess cauldrons and ingredients. Alchemy is a two-step process: first, grind herbs and reagents into powder using a Mortar and Pestle (place it in a bag slot to access its combine menu); then combine the powder with a vial inside a cauldron to produce the final product. Temporary cauldrons are player-crafted through Blacksmithing, while a permanent one can be bought from the alchemy vendor. Blacksmithing-made cauldrons have more combine slots at higher tiers: Copper (6), Tin (7), Bronze (8), Iron (9), Steel (10).

name: Disenchanting
note: Disenchanting runs backwards from a normal recipe: you feed in a MAGIC item (the card's "Components") and get a variable amount of magic powder/essence back, not a fixed crafted result. Since the output is a range rather than a single fixed item+quantity, each card's name describes both possible outputs and their ranges instead of naming one crafted item. You'll need a Disenchanting Cube (placed in a bag slot) to actually do this — put the MAGIC item in the cube and click Combine. As with any tradeskill there's a chance to fail, which destroys the item with no powder/essence gained. The quality of the powder produced seems tied to the item being disenchanted, though it's unclear whether that's the number of attributes on the item, its "item level", or something else. Any item tagged MAGIC can be fed into the cube this way — the recipes below aren't a specific checklist of disenchantable armor/weapons (that would be almost every MAGIC item in the game), they're the raw magic-essence materials (the Frond and Crystallized Magic tiers) that can also be disenchanted the same way, sorted here by tier from lowest to highest.

name: Herbalism
note: Herbalism Zaarid is the herbalist trainer located in the Sojourner's Camp in Night Harbor. Next to him is an herbalist which sells the required Sickle, the optional Herb Pouch, and will buy all excess herbs. Early leveling from 1-25 can be done by harvesting Lionleaf and Ghost Poppy in Night Harbor outside of North Gate and West Gate. The next tier of leveling can be done by harvesting Ironroot, Nomad's Grace, and Whispering Sage in Shaded Dunes, in the Ashira Oasis behind the sand wall illusion.

name: Lumberjacking
note: To begin lumberjacking, equip a Lumberjack Axe and right-click on a wood pile. These are often found next to trees. This will give you Wood.

name: Tanning
note: Tanning has no crafted recipes — instead, any tier-appropriate pelt is dropped into a tanning vat and processed directly into scrap material. Each card below shows one pelt-to-scrap conversion rather than a traditional recipe.
